business.headline: upGrad to acquire Unacademy in all-stock deal, Gaurav Munjal to remain CEOThe acquisition signals a major consolidation in India's online education space, bringing together two leading edtech players under a unified, AI-driven growth strategy.

BNW's Smita Nair sees strong demand for UAE luxury homesSpeaking at a session at the India Today Conclave, Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong even as parts of the mid-market segment show caution.

Luxury, waterfront homes driving UAE real estate demand: BNW's Smita NairSpeaking at a breakaway session at the India Today Conclave 2026, Smita Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong, particularly among international investors.

Small teams will soon disrupt big companies in AI era: Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, the founder of OpenExO and founding executive director of Singularity University said rapid advances in AI are changing how companies operate and compete.

The next $1 trillion company could have only 5 employees: Singularity's Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Salim Ismail, founding executive director of Singularity University and founder of OpenExO, said the rapid acceleration of technologies such as artificial intelligence is changing the economics of innovation and giving small teams unprecedented power to disrupt large corporations.

India a bright spot amid global uncertainty, says AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large population, strong domestic demand and long-term infrastructure needs make it one of the most promising markets globally, says Arvind Tiku.
Renewables, urbanisation and demand to power India's growth: AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large domestic market and young population create strong long-term growth potential, says Arvind Tiku.

If valuables vanish from your locker, can insurance cover the loss?This is the second story in Lock & Key, a series examining how India's bank locker system works and what customers should know before trusting their valuables to a steel vault.

Either I collapse or get up and go: Anil Agarwal reflects on loss of his sonVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said the death of his son Agnivesh has strengthened his resolve to continue working for India, urging opportunities for youth and calling for world-class education at home.

ED arrests 2 in Rs 183-crore online ponzi scam; accused returned from DubaiThe case was registered in December 2024 in Thane, accusing Sudhir Kotadiya, Umang Kotadiya, Jaysukh Sakhariya and others of running a fraudulent digital investment scheme that allegedly collected large sums from investors in Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Highlights: Two vessels carrying 92,700 metric tons of LPG en route to India, govt assuresLPG Crisis Highlights: Amid reports of commercial LPG cylinder shortage, Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge slammed the Centre, calling the crisis a result of the BJP government's "disastrous policy" and questioned PM Narendra Modi's foreign policy moves. Meanwhile, the shortage is beginning to affect services on the ground as well—food for the public at the "Didi Ki Rasoi" run by Jeevika didis at Rohtas Sadar Hospital has been stopped due to lack of LPG cylinders, and patient meals could also be affected if supply is not restored. Meanwhile, the government has assured that two vessels named Nanda Devi and Shivalik are en route to India with 92,700 metric tons of LPG cylinders.

Which four shipping routes control global trade? Karan Adani breaks it downKaran Adani explained that a large part of global shipping passes through four critical choke points. These routes act as key gateways for international trade and energy transport.

How is the global energy landscape changing? Karan Adani explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Karan Adani said the world has witnessed repeated disruptions in supply chains over the past few years, forcing countries to rethink how they manage energy and trade.

After Air India, IndiGo imposes fuel surcharge amid Middle East crisisThe energy crisis triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East is all set to make your air travel costlier. India's biggest domestic airline, IndiGo, on Friday announced it will impose Rs 425 to Rs 2,300 fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights from Friday (March 14).

Energy, ports and materials define Adani Group's strategy: Karan AdaniKaran Adani emphasised that the group's business expansion is not random but guided by long-term economic changes. Energy transition, digital infrastructure and industrial materials are areas expected to grow significantly in the coming years.

Adani Ports ready to handle energy cargo amid global disruptions: Karan AdaniSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Karan Adani, Managing Director of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd said supply chains worldwide have faced repeated shocks in recent years, forcing countries to rethink their dependence on highly globalised trade networks.

In an AI-driven world, storytelling and creativity will matter most: Christina WallaceWallace spoke about the growing impact of artificial intelligence. While AI will change many jobs, she believes human creativity will remain essential.

Storytelling will be the most valuable skill in the AI era: Christina WallaceOne skill that Wallace believes will become even more important in the age of AI is storytelling. While machines can produce data and structured information, they struggle to capture human emotion and experiences.

IndiGo, Air India seek tax relief as airspace bans hit operations, raise costIndiGo and Air India have urged the government to cut aviation fuel taxes and reduce private airport charges as the Iran conflict and Pakistan airspace ban force longer routes, raising operational costs and straining airline finances.
India's bond market must expand to finance Viksit Bharat goals, says Anurag MittalIndia will require massive investment to sustain growth, says Mittal.

entertainment.headline: 98th Oscars: One Battle After Another wins Best Picture at Academy Awards 2026 98th Oscars: One Battle After Another wins Best Picture at Academy Awards 2026

Jessie Buckley bags Best Actress for Hamnet, bursts into tears after Oscar win Jessie Buckley bagged the Best Actress award for Hamnet, making her the first Irish actor to win the honour. She was nominated alongside Emma Stone, Kate Hudson, Renate Reinsve and Rose Byrne.

Oscars 2026: Conan O'Brien ribs Timothee Chalemet over ballet-opera row Conan O'Brien's opening segment at the 2026 Oscars featured a playful jab at Timothee Chalamet regarding his recent comments on ballet and opera. Chalamet's reaction to the monologue quickly gained attention online.

Sinners cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw makes Oscar history Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history at the Oscars by becoming the first woman and woman of colour to win Best Cinematography for Sinners. Her win is a landmark achievement in a male-dominated field.

Oscars 2026 In Memoriam: Dharmendra left out; Rob Reiner, Catherine O'Hara honoured The 2026 Oscars In Memoriam segment sparked disappointment among Indian cinema fans following the omission of legendary actor Dharmendra. Several Hollywood icons including Rob Reiner and Catherine O'Hara received tributes.

Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans reunite at Oscars ahead of Avengers: Doomsday Hollywood witnessed a delightful Marvel reunion as Robert Downey Jr and Chris Evans shared the stage at the 98th Academy Awards, blending humour with nostalgia.

Oscars 2026: Rare tie in Best Live Action Short surprises all At the 2026 Oscars, both The Singers and Two People Exchanging Saliva were jointly awarded Best Live Action Short, marking the first tie at the ceremony since 2013.

Oscars 2026: Priyanka-Nick just made the red carpet a romantic hotspot. See pics Priyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas swooned everyone with their appearance on the red carpet at the Academy Awards 2026. The two brought glamour and served major couple goals to the prestigious ceremony.

98th Oscars winners list: Sinners, F1 bag awards for Best Score, Sound The 98th Academy Awards are currently happening at the Dolby Theatre with Conan O'Brien hosting the grand event. While Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress, Sean Penn bagged Best Supporting Actor.

Peaky Blinders The Immortal Man on OTT: When and where to watch the film Cillian Murphy's Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man is set for its OTT release. Here's when the crime-drama arrives on streaming. Check out the cast and story details.

BAFTA winner Boong becomes first Manipuri film to earn over Rs 1 crore Lakshmipriya Devi's Boong became the first Manipuri film to earn over Rs 1 crore in India. The film was theatrically released on March 6.

Oscars 2026: Which Indian stars have been presenters at the gala? See here As Priyanka Chopra gets ready to present at the Oscars 2026 again this year, we look at Indians who represented India at the Academy Awards.

Sunil Grover's viral Kader Khan tribute floors Varun Dhawan, Bhumi Pednekar. Watch Varun Dhawan, Bhumi Pednekar, Ishaan Khatter and other celebrities showered praise on Sunil Grover's mimicry of late actor Kader Khan. Grover paid a special tribute to the legendary actor on The Great Indian Kapil Show Season 4.

Films will not be first priority: Raashii Khanna on US-Iran conflict fallout Raashii Khanna raised concerns regarding the impact of the Iran-US conflict on cinema. She reflected on how films will not be the first priority if the situation doesn't de-escalate.

Allu Arjun thanks Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu for blessing Allu Sirish-Nayanika Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu extended his blessings to newly-weds Allu Sirish and Nayanika Reddy during a visit to their family residence. Several pictures of the newly-weds greeting Reddy emerged online. Sirish's brother, Allu Arjun, has thanked the Andhra CM for his gesture.

Rashmika's special birthday wish for Anand Deverakonda: May we always be crazy Rashmika Mandanna shared a special message on brother-in-law Deverakonda's 34th birthday. She shared a throwback video with Anand where the duo is seen dancing to the viral Malayalam track Kalyani.

Heeseung's ENHYPEN exit sparks outrage: K-pop's solo vs group struggle continues ENHYPEN's Heeseung exit sparks global outrage as millions sign a petition for his return, reigniting debate on whether K-pop idols can balance solo and group careers.

David Dhawan defends Govinda over arriving late: He completes his work and leaves Director David Dhawan defended Bollywood star Govinda over arriving late on set. Dhawan recalled his working experience with the actor and praised his work ethic.

Alia Bhatt launches children's production label on birthday, 3 projects underway On her 33rd birthday today, Alia Bhatt announced her children's production company. Eternal Kids. The new initiative already has three projects in the pipeline, she said.

general news.headline: 10 killed in massive fire at state-run hospital in Cuttack; short circuit suspectedTen patients were killed and seven others injured in a massive fire at SCB Medical College and Hospital in Odisha's Cuttack. A short circuit in the ICU's AC or medical equipment is suspected to have caused the blaze.

Massive fire breaks out near Dubai airport after drone attack, flight ops suspended  Live UpdatesA drone strike hit a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport, sparking a massive fire that briefly halted flight operations.

Leave, it's okay: Harish Rana's family bids goodbye before his dignified deathAfter 13 years in a coma, Harish Rana was shifted to AIIMS for court-approved passive euthanasia. Doctors will gradually withdraw life support under supervision, ending his prolonged vegetative state.

Oscars 2026 live updates: Michael B Jordan, Jessie Buckley win top honoursOscars 2026 live updates: The 98th Academy Awards have officially begun. Hosted by Connan O'Brien, the ceremony celebrates the best films of 2025 with a star-studded ceremony, global broadcast and high anticipation among cinephiles worldwide.

Oscars 2026: Priyanka-Nick just made the red carpet a romantic hotspot. See picsPriyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas swooned everyone with their appearance on the red carpet at the Academy Awards 2026. The two brought glamour and served major couple goals to the prestigious ceremony.

Ahura and Asura: Iran and India are brothers linked by Gods and sacred fireHandcrafted ByOur Editors When we look at Iran through the prism of religion and see a Shia Islamic country, we negate its thousands of years of rich pre-Islamic Persian culture. A dive into the world of Zoroastrianism and Vedas shows us how Indians and Iranians have been sharing languages, Gods, sciences and a sacred fire for thousands of years.

Oscars 2026: Conan O'Brien ribs Timothee Chalemet over ballet-opera rowConan O'Brien's opening segment at the 2026 Oscars featured a playful jab at Timothee Chalamet regarding his recent comments on ballet and opera. Chalamet's reaction to the monologue quickly gained attention online.

US plans to announce multi-nation coalition to escort ships through Hormuz: WSJSeveral countries, including some very close allies of the US, have appeared evasive on US President Donald Trump's open call to nations to send warships to enable container vessels to pass through the war-hit Strait of Hormuz unharmed.

Oscars 2026: Timothee-Kylie, Pedro Pascal, Demi Moore, celebs light up red carpetThe biggest stars assembled at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles to celebrate cinema, and classic Hollywood glamour got on a modern twist. Have a look at the pictures of the best dressed stars on the Oscars 2026 red carpet.
98th Oscars: One Battle After Another wins Best Picture at Academy Awards 202698th Oscars: One Battle After Another wins Best Picture at Academy Awards 2026

Iran embassy fund appeal draws big India response; cash sought amid transfer issueThe Iranian embassy thanked Indian citizens who had expressed interest in sending humanitarian assistance to people affected by the conflict, but said technical issues had made it difficult to receive donations through online transfers to its bank account.

India's GPS is dying: NavIC satellite network down to 3 satellitesNavIC, India's answer to GPS, has dropped below the minimum strength needed to function after a key satellite's atomic clock failed. With only three satellites left and replacements delayed, the system is in trouble.

Isha Ambani brings spring to the Oscars 2026 red carpet in vintage ValentinoThe gown that Isha Ambani chose for the Oscars 2026 night is from Maison Valentino. The particular piece is from their Fall 2006 Couture Collection.

Shubman Gill, Smriti Mandhana win BCCI's Cricketer of the Year awardsNaman Awards 2026: On Sunday, March 15, Shubman Gill won the Polly Umrigar Award for Best International Men's Cricketer of the Year, while Smriti Mandhana was named Women's Cricketer of the Year.

Oscars 2026 In Memoriam: Dharmendra left out; Rob Reiner, Catherine O'Hara honouredThe 2026 Oscars In Memoriam segment sparked disappointment among Indian cinema fans following the omission of legendary actor Dharmendra. Several Hollywood icons including Rob Reiner and Catherine O'Hara received tributes.

Congress MLAs offered Rs 5 crore to cross vote in Rajya Sabha: DK ShivakumarTwo Odisha Congress MLAs were offered Rs 5 crore each to cross vote in Rajya Sabha elections, DK Shivakumar alleged. Police have arrested two suspects, seized a cheque, and launched an investigation into the incident.

Another strait to get shut after Hormuz? Houthis ready to pull trigger in Iran warWhile the Strait of Hormuz is choked amid the war on Iran, the focus is now on another strait in the region, the Bab el-Mandeb. The Yemeni Houthi group, part of Tehran's "Axis of Resistance", has warned its "fingers are on the trigger". The Houthis joining the war could choke yet another crucial artery of global trade.

They say I'm what? Netanyahu mocks death rumours while sipping coffee in new videoIsraeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu responded directly to his death rumours by releasing a new video intended to show he was alive and continuing his duties.

Beyond the ballot: The battlegrounds that will decide Bengal 2026With over 7.3 crore voters, including 1.31 crore youth, West Bengal's 2026 elections hinge on six critical regions, where welfare schemes, identity politics, and economic concerns will determine if TMC or BJP dominates the state.

Bengal to vote in 2 phases, Kerala and Assam polls on April 9. Result on May 4Results for West Bengal, Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry will be declared on May 4 as India heads into a politically charged electoral season that will be a test for Brand Modi against regional satraps.

When wars entered the kitchen: How global conflicts changed what we eatHandcrafted ByOur Editors From canned food to instant coffee, many everyday staples were born not in kitchens but on battlefields.

Bihar Rajya Sabha polls today; hotel politics on eve of voteAhead of voting for five Rajya Sabha seats in Bihar on March 16, parties have begun consolidating MLAs, with the Grand Alliance keeping its legislators together in Patna.

An AI-proof, recession-proof career, with blessings from templesThere is seemingly a job that AI can't take away, and it might boom during recession. Wondering what it is? It's related to India's temple economy, and is coming up as a new career path. Courses in temple management are drawing Gen Z students and retirees alike.

Jessie Buckley bags Best Actress for Hamnet, bursts into tears after Oscar winJessie Buckley bagged the Best Actress award for Hamnet, making her the first Irish actor to win the honour. She was nominated alongside Emma Stone, Kate Hudson, Renate Reinsve and Rose Byrne.

sports.headline: Sinner outlasts Medvedev in gritty Indian Wells final, joins hard-court elites Jannik Sinner edged Daniil Medvedev in a tense Indian Wells final to complete a rare hard-court Masters sweep. The Italian joined Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer as the only men to win all six ATP Masters 1000 hard-court titles.

Pakistan slump further after T20 World Cup debacle, lose ODI series to Bangladesh BAN vs PAK, 3rd ODI: Bangladesh beat Pakistan by 11 runs in the third and final ODI on Sunday at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium to win the series 2-1.

Samson reveals Sooryavanshi's carefree chat with Dravid: Pehla hi uda denge During the Naman Awards, Sanju Samson revealed that during IPL 2025, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi confidently told Rahul Dravid he would simply play his natural game, even planning to go aerial on the first opportunity.

Gulveer Singh shatters national record with bronze in New York City half marathon India's Gulveer Singh made history at the NYC Half Marathon 2026, winning bronze and breaking the national men's half-marathon record with a time of 59:42, becoming the first Indian to run under one hour.

Shubman Gill, Smriti Mandhana win BCCI's Cricketer of the Year awards Naman Awards 2026: On Sunday, March 15, Shubman Gill won the Polly Umrigar Award for Best International Men's Cricketer of the Year, while Smriti Mandhana was named Women's Cricketer of the Year.

BCCI felicitates India's 5 World Cup-winning teams in New Delhi The BCCI honoured India's five World Cup-winning teams at the Naman Awards 2026 in New Delhi, with the U19 women's, U19 men's, Champions Trophy, Women's World Cup and Men's T20 World Cup-winning sides all being felicitated.

Done with losing big finals: Sabalenka eyes revenge against Rybakina at Indian Wells Indian Wells: World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka has said she will give it her best shot when she takes on Elena Rybakina in Sunday's final. Sabalenka has lost each of her last four finals against Rybakina.

Fuming Max Verstappen tears into new F1 era after Shanghai GP retirement Max Verstappen launched a fierce attack on Formula One's new regulations after retiring from the Chinese Grand Prix with a power unit failure, calling the rules "fundamentally flawed" and warning they could ruin the sport.

Amelia Kerr equals McCullum in elite list, continues dream run as New Zealand captain NZW vs SAW: Amelia Kerr continued her red-hot run as captain, smashing 78 off 44 balls against South Africa, equalling Brendon McCullum and leading the hosts to an 80-run win in the first T20I.

Gambhir's 2014 IPL nightmare helped Abhishek bounce back in T20 World Cup final Gautam Gambhir reveals he used his own four-duck IPL 2014 nightmare to back Abhishek Sharma through three consecutive ducks before his match-winning 52 in the T20 World Cup final.

South Africa twist knife on New Zealand's T20 World Cup heartbreak with 7-wicket rout South Africa's debutants bowled New Zealand out for 91 at Bay Oval to win the first T20I by seven wickets, piling more misery on the World Cup finalists just days after their final defeat.

Is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi riding on luck? Ex-India star predicts tougher IPL 2026IPL 2026: Former India cricketer Robin Uthappa believes young batting sensation Vaibhav Suryavanshi will face a tougher challenge in IPL 2026. The 14-year-old prodigy impressed last season, but Uthappa expects bowlers to test him more this year.

Pakistan U19 coach Sarfaraz Ahmed announces retirement from international cricketSarfaraz Ahmed has retired from international cricket, bringing the curtain down on a decorated career for Pakistan in which he scored 6,164 runs and led the team to the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy title.

F1 Shanghai GP: Mercedes' Antonelli takes first win, Hamilton secures first podiumKimi Antonelli won the 2026 Chinese Grand Prix for Mercedes, with Lewis Hamilton taking his first Ferrari podium. McLaren's Norris and Piastri both failed to start in Shanghai.

KKR should not have let Shreyas Iyer go: Anil Kumble slams team's retention policyAnil Kumble believes Kolkata Knight Riders weakened their own title-winning core by releasing Shreyas Iyer and Phil Salt after their 2024 triumph. The former India captain criticised the franchise's inconsistent retention strategy.

New kit in 7 hours: Indian football body hits back at critics over Women's Asian Cup rowAIFF deputy secretary general M Satyanarayan says the federation replaced the Indian women's team's ill-fitting AFC Women's Asian Cup kits within seven hours, but the effort went unrecognised.

Robot, national treasure: Suryakumar doesn't hold back on his praise for BumrahT20 World Cup 2026: Suryakumar Yadav called Jasprit Bumrah a "robot" and "national treasure" after his match-winning 4/15 in the T20 World Cup final helped India defend their title against New Zealand.

The Hands of God that shaped Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup redemptionSanju Samson began the year battling doubt and uncertainty. Months later, he stood as India's T20 World Cup hero, lifted by belief, mentorship and a support system that helped him rediscover the fearless batter within.

3 Iran women's football team members head home after rejecting Australia asylumThree members of Iran's women's football team who had accepted humanitarian visas in Australia have now chosen to return home. Their departure leaves only three players from the initial group who decided to stay behind.

Rohit Sharma hopeful India will extend cricket dominance: T20 World Cup just a startRohit Sharma believes India's recent success in ICC tournaments could be the start of a strong phase for the national teams. The India captain praised both men's and women's teams and hopes the winning run continues.

Watch: Real Madrid's Arda Guler ties LaLiga record with stunning 74-yard goalArda Guler lit up the Santiago Bernabeu with a sensational long-range strike as Real Madrid beat Elche. The Turkish youngster's outrageous effort from distance matched the longest goal ever scored in LaLiga history.

Formula 1 cancel Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix due to Middle East warFormula 1 has cancelled the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix scheduled for April as tensions escalate in West Asia. Safety concerns linked to the Iran conflict forced the sport to drop both races from the 2026 calendar.

Medvedev ends Alcaraz's winning streak with Indian Wells semi-finals winDaniil Medvedev snapped Carlos Alcaraz's unbeaten run in 2026 with a clinical straight-sets win in the Indian Wells semi-finals. The Russian will now meet Jannik Sinner in the title clash after the Italian defeated Alexander Zverev.

Show a little trust: Suryakumar reveals how a phone call changed Ishan Kishan's fateSuryakumar Yadav revealed how a phone call and a little faith helped Ishan Kishan stage a sensational comeback, earning him a spot in India's Men's T20 World Cup squad. Kishan capitalised on the opportunity, scoring 317 runs in nine matches.

Kuldeep Yadav marries longtime girlfriend Vanshika in a grand Mussoorie ceremonyIndian cricketer Kuldeep Yadav married his childhood friend, Vanshika Singh, on March 14 in a grand ceremony held in Mussoorie. The celebrations were even more special for Kuldeep, coming shortly after India's T20 World Cup 2026 triumph.

New Zealand series gave enough petrol in my tank to go extra mile: Sanju SamsonSpeaking at the India Today Conclave, Sanju Samson credited the T20I series against New Zealand for preparing him to succeed on the global stage. After scoring just 46 runs across five games in the bilateral series, Samson went on to earn the Player of the Tournament award at the T20 World Cup.

Pakistan needs to move on from scapegoat culture: National selectorPakistan national selector Aaqib Javed believes that the country and the country's cricket board need to move on from scapegoating culture to move forward in international cricket.

Surya, Gambhir and Shah visit Siddhivinayak temple with ICC trophySuryakumar Yadav, Gautam Gambhir and Jay Shah carried India's T20 World Cup trophy to Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple. Earlier, they had also taken the trophy to a Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad after India's title triumph.

Abhishek Sharma recalls father backing him amid poor form: He tried puja and paathSpeaking at the India Today Conclave in New Delhi, Abhishek Sharma revealed how his father and family helped him overcome a difficult T20 World Cup campaign, during which he battled poor form before scoring a match-winning 18-ball fifty in the final against New Zealand.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
